channel.title: Nature Communications
channel.description:
Nature Communications is an open access, multidisciplinary journal dedicated to publishing high-quality research in all areas of the biological, health, physical, chemical and Earth sciences. Papers published by the journal aim to represent important advances of significance to specialists within each field.

We are committed to providing an efficient service for both authors and readers. Our team of independent editors make rapid and fair publication decisions. Prompt dissemination of accepted papers to a wide readership and beyond is achieved through a programme of continuous online publication.
channel.dc:rights: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.
channel.prism:copyright: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.

item.title: [8] Persistent paramagnons in high-temperature infinite-layer nickelate superconductors
item.content:encoded:
Last authors: A. Ariando, Qisi Wang

Authors: Yujie Yan, Ying Chan, Xunyang Hong, et al.

Score 8/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: RIXS study of nickelate superconductors is directly relevant to strongly correlated electron systems.

Tags: #RIXS #nickelate #superconductivity #correlated_electrons

Abstract: Nature Communications, Published online: 20 May 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-73083-3 The authors report a resonant inelastic x-ray scattering study of superconducting Sm-based infinite-layer nickelate thin films. Despite the two-fold enhancement of Tc in the Sm-based nickelates compared to their Pr-based counterparts, they find that the effective in-plane exchange coupling strength is reduced by approximately 20%.
item.dc:title: [8] Persistent paramagnons in high-temperature infinite-layer nickelate superconductors
item.dc:creator: Last authors: A. Ariando, Qisi Wang; Authors: Yujie Yan, Ying Chan, Xunyang Hong, et al.

Xingjiang Zhou
Zhihai Zhu
Liang Si
Mirian Garcia-Fernandez
Ke-Jin Zhou

Di-Jing Huang

A. Ariando
Qisi Wang
item.description:
Last authors: A. Ariando, Qisi Wang

Authors: Yujie Yan, Ying Chan, Xunyang Hong, et al.

Score 8/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: RIXS study of nickelate superconductors is directly relevant to strongly correlated electron systems.

Tags: #RIXS #nickelate #superconductivity #correlated_electrons

Abstract: Nature Communications, Published online: 20 May 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-73083-3 The authors report a resonant inelastic x-ray scattering study of superconducting Sm-based infinite-layer nickelate thin films. Despite the two-fold enhancement of Tc in the Sm-based nickelates compared to their Pr-based counterparts, they find that the effective in-plane exchange coupling strength is reduced by approximately 20%.
